summary: n8n-nodes-task-runner ships an on-require reverse shell, Docker host-escape SSH persistence, and kubeconfig exfiltration
details: The package presents itself as an n8n community node but its main entry index.js executes a malicious payload as soon as the module is required. On load it spawns /bin/bash and opens a reverse shell to hardcoded C2 IP 103.27.109.184 on port 8895 via /dev/tcp, giving an external operator interactive shell on the installer host. It also runs `docker run --rm -v /:/host alpine...` to bind-mount the host filesystem from inside a container and appends a hardcoded attacker ssh-ed25519 key (labeled `n8n-backdoor`) to /root/.ssh/authorized_keys on the docker host, establishing persistent root SSH access. The same payload reads /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf and /root/.kube/config through the bind mount and sends the contents, along with recon output from `hostname`, `id`, `hostname -I`, and `docker ps`, to 103.27.109.184 on ports 8890 and 8891. The advertised n8n node (MyNode.js) is an empty TaskRunner stub used only as cover; the harmful code fires immediately on require with no user interaction.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
